title: The Fox And Huntsman.
content:
Hard 'tis on a fox's traces

    To arrive, midst forest-glades;
    Hopeless utterly the chase is,

    If his flight the huntsman aids.

    And so 'tis with many a wonder,


    (Why A B make Ab in fact,)
    Over which we gape and blunder,

    And our head and brains distract.
meaning:
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Fox And Huntsman."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
